title: Looking Back: William McKinley Monument – Richmond Review/Sunset Beacon
content: # Richmond Review/Sunset Beacon. ## News for San Francisco's Richmond and Sunset Districts. By San Francisco Richmond ReView on  • ( Leave a comment ). San Franciscans reacted so strongly to the assassination that the public spontaneously donated funds for a monument, according to a 1904 issue of the San Francisco Call newspaper detailing the monument’s unveiling. **The likeness of William McKinley on the monument dedicated to him is small compared to a female figure atop the monument representing the “Republic.”** ­­. He was upset that the McKinley Tariff was modified in the Senate to allow for many Latin American countries to export sugar to the U.S, inadvertently creating more competition for the Kingdom of Hawaii’s sugar export to the U.S. This contributed to the collapse of the Hawaiian monarchy and annexation by the U.S, according to Alfred L.

title: Why the McKinley Monument - by Susan Freinkel
content: Until Donald Trump began talking up William McKinley, I hadn’t given the McKinley monument in the Panhandle much thought. There’s so much in the park I didn’t consider until I started this newsletter.). Like Trump, McKinley helped consolidate Republican hold on power; cut back the civil service; and was a master at using the press, publicity and a then-new communications technology, the telephone, in his campaigns and presidency. In the view of the San Francisco Call, that shift “changed the front of this republic from the Atlantic to the Pacific.” What an exciting prospect for San Francisco, then west coast’s the largest west. But the likeliest reason for the monument — just one of four McKinley memorials in the country — is a visit McKinley made here in May, 1901, his one and only time in the city. It occurred as part of “a six week victory lap around the country to inaugurate his second term” with San Francisco in the west and Buffalo in the east as the trip’s highlights.

title: McKinley Monument: The Republic, San Francisco, California
content: The stern face of America's martyred 25th President glowers from a bronze medallion set into the pedestal base of this towering monument.

title: McKinley Presidential Library and Museum (Canton) - Visitor Information & Reviews
content: # McKinley Presidential Library and Museum. 5. Current: McKinley Presidential Library and Museum. ## McKinley Presidential Library and Museum and its collection. The William McKinley Presidential Library and Museum is a significant historical site dedicated to the 25th U.S. president, William McKinley. The museum is a repository of the largest collection of McKinley artifacts in the world, providing a comprehensive chronicle of the president's life and career. ### The McKinley Artifacts Collection. The museum houses the world's largest collection of artifacts related to President McKinley. This comprehensive collection offers visitors a unique insight into the life and times of the 25th U.S. president. #**4** History & Anthropology in Canton   #**3** Person & artist museums in Canton   #**108** History & Anthropology in Ohio   #**12** Animal museums in Ohio   #**4** Astronomy museums in Ohio   #**5** Geology museums in Ohio   #**82** Historic houses in Ohio   #**41** Person & artist museums in Ohio   #**115** Astronomy museums in United States. ### Images of McKinley Presidential Library and Museum. The McKinley Presidential Library and Museum in Canton, Ohio, offers visitors a comprehensive look at the life and presidency of William McKinley.
